blurb:
Jacquelyn came into rescue from animal control, landed in a foster home with three other dogs, and has been living her best life ever since. She's two years old, about 58 pounds, and her foster describes her as lovable, huggable, and genuinely fun to be around. She knows her commands, loves to play, and will absolutely wiggle her way into the center of your attention — not in a pushy way, just in a "this is my person and I intend to be near them" kind of way.

She does have a congenital kidney defect, which sounds scarier than it is. Her foster says it's easily managed — just a few regular potty breaks during the day, no more than any normal dog needs. She sleeps through the night without a single trip outside. She's living fully and happily, and her foster wants her family to know that going in. She'd do best with someone who works from home or has a flexible schedule, and she's great with other friendly dogs. No cats, and no toddlers she might accidentally bowl over with enthusiasm.

Mostly Mutts is a rescue based in Kennesaw. Visit their website to submit an inquiry and set up a meet with Jacquelyn.
shelter_hours: See website for current hours: mostlymutts.org
scoring_notes:
• The 'health condition that isn't scary' framing is a powerful editorial hook that challenges reader assumptions and builds empathy
• Foster's insistence on full transparency about the kidney defect adds credibility and human warmth that resonates in newsletter format
• Work-from-home requirement and cat/toddler restrictions narrow the pool meaningfully, suggesting she may have been waiting longer than average

blurb:
Madelyn came in from animal control at about 2.5 months old, went straight into a foster home, and has recently moved to the Kitty Aquarium at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. She weighs about 2.5 pounds right now, which means she is absolutely tiny, and she is apparently very aware of how fun that is.

Her foster described her as curious and super playful, the kind of kitten who treats every corner of a room like it needs investigating and every toy like it was invented specifically for her. She's been litter trained since the start, got along well with the cats she's lived with, and was slowly warming up to a calm dog in her foster home. She likes people, she likes other cats, and she seems pretty enthusiastic about life in general.

If you have a cat at home who's been flying solo for a while, Madelyn is the kind of addition that tends to make everyone's life more interesting. She's ready to meet you at Mostly Mutts on Kennesaw Due West Road. You can visit her in person at the Kitty Aquarium or start the process through their website.
shelter_address: 3832 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Tiny, litter-trained, people-friendly kitten with near-universal compatibility is an exceptionally easy adoption pitch
• Straightforward rescue-to-foster-to-floor-availability trajectory offers a clean, feel-good story with broad reader appeal
• Recently moved to the shelter floor suggesting a relatively short wait — lower urgency but high immediate adoptability

blurb:
Josie has floppy ears, a nubby little tail, and more energy than her last family knew what to do with. She's six years old, 35 pounds, a hound mix, and she was returned not because anything was wrong with her — her energy level just wasn't the right fit. So now she's looking for someone who actually wants an adventure buddy. Think trail walks, active weekends, a person who doesn't mind a dog that keeps up.

Here's the flip side: her foster says her cuddle level matches her energy level. So after all that, she wants to be on the couch with you. She completed the Mostly Mutts prison training program, loves people, and even warmed up during her shelter bath enough to start giving kisses by the end of it. She does some mouthy, playful nibbling — never mean, just enthusiastic — so older kids around 10 and up would be a better fit than little ones.

She'd prefer to be your only dog, and cats are a no. If you've got the energy and a couch to share at the end of the day, go meet Josie at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. You can submit an inquiry through their website to get started.
shelter_hours: See website for current hours: mostlymutts.org
scoring_notes:
• Returned for being 'too much dog' is a deeply relatable and redemptive story that will resonate with active readers looking for a real companion
• Prison training program credential plus the bath-kissing detail gives editors two vivid, shareable moments in a single blurb
• A six-year-old returned dog with an only-dog, no-cat profile has likely been waiting significantly — high urgency for the right reader

blurb:
Her foster family calls her the best cat ever, and they mean it. Gina is about 11 months old, barely 5 pounds, and fully committed to staying that way. She came in with a litter of siblings, got adopted, and then came back after some stomach issues started showing up. After months of vet care and food changes, she's doing well, though she does still vomit occasionally and the cause hasn't been pinned down. That's the honest version, and it's worth knowing going in.

What's also worth knowing: none of that has touched her personality. She's playful, affectionate, and apparently great with people, other cats, and even dogs she's met on a leash. Her foster says she's independent enough to do her own thing but absolutely loves to snuggle when she's in the mood. She has maintained full kitten energy at nearly a year old, which is exactly as delightful as it sounds.

Because she's on a special diet, a home where she's the only cat is easiest, but a separate feeding setup with another laid-back cat could absolutely work too. If you've been on the fence about adding a cat to your home, Gina is a pretty compelling reason to get off it. She's at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Submit an adoption inquiry through their website to get started.
shelter_address: 3832 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Rare transparency about health history paired with glowing foster praise creates rare reader trust and emotional investment
• Adopted-and-returned arc with a happy recovery ending is a compelling redemption narrative tailor-made for newsletter readers
• Months of vet care and a second stint in rescue signal meaningful time waiting — urgency to find her a permanent home is real

blurb:
Yasmeen is eight years old, came from animal control, went through a prison training program, landed in a foster home, and her foster mom says whoever adopts her is going to hit the jackpot. That's the kind of endorsement that means something.

She takes a little time to trust new people — that's just who she is. But the training program showed it doesn't take long. Once she knows you, she's loyal and loving and will protect her people like that's her whole job. She's good with cats and has gotten along with other dogs after a quick initial sizing-up period. She'd do best with dog-savvy kids 15 and up, and it's recommended that new people meet her outside the home first rather than walking straight into her space — a small adjustment that makes a big difference for her.

Yasmeen is a dog that needs the right fit, not a difficult one. If you're patient, consistent, and looking for a devoted companion who takes her loyalty seriously, she's worth going to meet. Reach out to Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw through their website to set up an introduction.
shelter_hours: See website for current hours: mostlymutts.org
scoring_notes:
• Eight years old, from animal control, through a prison program, into foster — the layered journey makes her the most narratively rich candidate in the group
• Foster's 'jackpot' endorsement combined with nuanced trust-building needs gives editors a story about earning loyalty that readers will remember
• Age, specific kid and intro requirements, and animal-control origins together strongly suggest an extended wait — this is the highest-urgency feature candidate

blurb:
Malik is 2.5 months old, about 2 pounds, and has apparently already decided that toys exist to be conquered and laps exist to be warmed. He came into Mostly Mutts from animal control alongside his friend Madelyn, spent time in a foster home, and recently moved to the Kitty Aquarium at the shelter. He is ready for the next chapter.

His foster described him as curious and super playful, which tracks for a kitten his age, but also just genuinely sweet. He's good with other cats, was slowly getting comfortable around a calm dog in his foster home, and keeps his space clean on his own. He likes people, he likes toys, and based on everything his foster said, he's pretty easy to be around.

At 2 pounds, he's going to need a little time to grow into himself, but his personality is already fully formed. If you've got room in your home and maybe another cat who'd enjoy some company, Malik is worth a visit. He's living at Mostly Mutts on Kennesaw Due West Road right now. Stop by to meet him or submit an inquiry through their website.
shelter_address: 3832 Kennesaw Due West Rd NW, Kennesaw, GA 30152
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Easygoing, playful, litter-trained kitten with strong social skills makes for one of the most adoptable candidates in the group
• Backstory is thin and mirrors Madelyn's closely, limiting differentiation and newsletter novelty
• Arrived recently alongside Madelyn and just transitioned to the shelter floor, indicating minimal time waiting
